meta.description: Tracks wildfire activity intersecting Ontario rail corridors — service suspensions, crew evacuations, and TDG-relevant exposure (trains carrying dangerous goods halted or rerouted due to fire).
meta.status: seeded manually — automated CWFIS/OPP correlation not yet wired up, see scraper/railwatch_firewatch.py

data_sources_planned.name: CWFIS (Canadian Wildland Fire Information System, NRCan)
data_sources_planned.what: Active fire hotspots (MODIS/VIIRS) and fire perimeter polygons

data_sources_planned.name: Ontario Ministry of Natural Resources — active fire list
data_sources_planned.what: Fire numbers, status, size, location by district

incidents.headline: CN transcontinental main (Allanwater Sub) halted by wildfire near Armstrong, ON — crew evacuation, viral cab video

location.description: North of Thunder Bay, unorganized Kenora District

corridor.note: Armstrong is Mile 0.00 of the Allanwater Subdivision, which runs west to Sioux Lookout (Mile 138.90) and connects east to the Caramat Subdivision.

trains_affected.cargo_note: Reported by OPP as hauling combustible products; specific consist/placard detail not yet available through public channels
trains_affected.crew_status: Evacuated safely — confirmed by CN spokesperson to CTV News

evacuation_orders: Gakijiwanong Anishinaabe Nation (Lac La Croix First Nation)
Lac des Mille Lacs First Nation

media.description: Cab-view cellphone video from a CN crew member shows flames on both sides of the train, thick smoke, and the crew reacting to worsening conditions ("This could potentially overtake us"). Video went viral on social media starting ~13 Jul 2026, widely reported starting 15 Jul 2026.
media.note: Original social video not embedded here — RailWatch links to news coverage rather than rehosting user-generated footage. See sources below.

regional_context.conditions: Extreme heat and wildfire smoke advisory across northeastern/northwestern Ontario; humidity trapping smoke, yellowish sky

tdg_relevance.assessment: High — directly illustrates the transparency gap RailWatch documents: a viral video, not a public TDG disclosure, is how this community-adjacent hazmat exposure event became visible.
tdg_relevance.historical_precedent: TSB R09W0033 — 13 Feb 2009 CN derailment on this same subdivision (Robinson, ON, ~Mile 108.23) involving sodium chlorate (UN 1495) and propane (UN 1075) tank cars struck a wayside propane tank, causing fire and explosion. No injuries. Demonstrates recurring hazmat/fire risk on the Allanwater Sub specifically.
